title: Fostering Scheme
text:
Fostering Scheme - SupportGoWhere
Fostering Scheme
Share link
Provides a safe and nurturing family environment for children needing care and protection, offering love, stability, and essentials like food and shelter. Foster families create positive family experiences, demonstrating care and concern, with the aim to reunite children with their birth families once safe to do so.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Allows children in need to benefit from a nurturing home environment that facilitates their growth and enables them to fulfil their potential
Support services are available for foster families, including fostering allowance to defray the cost of caring for the child, training for foster parents, and support and guidance from Foster Care Worker and other professionals
Aims to continue building up a diverse pool of foster parents to meet the different needs of foster children
Apply now
Collapse all sections
About this support

Monthly fostering allowance of $1,100 for each foster child, or $1,500 for a child with special needs

Childcare subsidies with a monthly co-payment of $200 for full-day care and $150 for half-day care to be paid by foster parents

Student care subsidies with a monthly co-payment of $80 to be paid by foster parents for MSF-registered centres

Medical subsidies cover medical treatment at public healthcare institutions (polyclinics, public hospitals, and public specialist centres)

Foster children also receive subsidies at participating CHAS General Practitioners and dental clinics

Support and guidance from Foster Care Worker attached to every foster parent

Training courses conducted by Social Service Institute for new foster parents

Immediate support via an emergency hotline available 24/7

Support groups and respite care available

Foster parents enjoy childcare leave benefits, just as birth parents do under the Child Development Co-Savings Act and Employment Act

Who is this for?

At least 25 years old

Married and applying as a couple

Medically fit to care for children

Minimum secondary school education

Minimum monthly household income of $2,000 and per capita income of at least $700 (Retirees can apply to be assessed based on non-work sources of income and saving)

Provide a child-safe home environment, which includes commitment not to use physical punishment, and installation of window grilles for foster children below 13 years old

How to apply?

Apply online through the MSF Fostering Scheme portal:

Click "Apply"

Register or sign in

Fill in the application form

Upload supporting documents

MSF or an appointed agency will contact you within three working days

The assessment include:

Interview session(s)

Home Visit

Medical screenings

After approval:

Attend mandatory training sessions

Await foster child placement

Where can I find help?

View more details

MSF Consolidated Hotline: 1800-111-2222 
To reach the Fostering division, say "Foster" when prompted by the virtual assistant.

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

512 Thomson Rd

#11-00 MSF Building

Singapore 298136

Scheme last updated 15 Dec 2025
SUPPORT RECOMMENDER
Answer a few questions to find schemes and services you may be eligible for.
Check now
